description: Add a Librarian module for persistent cross-task learning via embeddings and SQLite vector search, then integrate it into the existing kickoff, Ralph loop, and review phases.

userStories.title: Add OPENAI_API_KEY and LIBRARIAN_ENABLED config
userStories.description: As a developer, I need config support for the Librarian feature so that it can be toggled on/off and use OpenAI embeddings.
acceptanceCriteria: Add OPENAI_API_KEY (optional string) to config schema in src/config.ts
Add LIBRARIAN_ENABLED (boolean, default false) to config schema
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: These are optional fields - the runner must still work without them.

userStories.title: Add learnings table to SQLite DB
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a learnings table so the Librarian can persist extracted knowledge across tasks.
acceptanceCriteria: Add learnings table to the migrate() function in src/db.ts with columns: id, content, embedding (BLOB), category, tags (JSON text), project_type, source_agent, source_repo, source_task_id, created_at, updated_at, superseded_by
Add indexes on category and project_type
Existing tables and migrations are not broken
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use the same idempotent CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS pattern as existing tables. embedding is a BLOB storing serialized Float32Array.

userStories.title: Create embeddings module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an embeddings module that calls OpenAI text-embedding-3-small to generate vector embeddings from text.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/librarian/embeddings.ts with an embed(text: string) function that returns a Float32Array
Uses OpenAI text-embedding-3-small via direct fetch (no SDK dependency)
Handles errors gracefully (returns null or throws descriptive error)
Reads OPENAI_API_KEY from config
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use native fetch - no new dependencies. Embedding dimension is 1536 for text-embedding-3-small.

userStories.title: Create vector store module with cosine similarity search
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a vector store module that stores embeddings in SQLite and performs cosine similarity search in-process.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/librarian/vectorstore.ts
Implement store(id, embedding) to save a Float32Array as a BLOB in the learnings table
Implement search(queryEmbedding, limit) that loads all embeddings, computes cosine similarity, and returns top N results with scores
Cosine similarity is computed correctly
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Corpus will be small (<10K entries), so in-memory cosine similarity is fine. Serialize Float32Array to/from Buffer for SQLite BLOB storage.

userStories.title: Create learnings DB CRUD module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need CRUD operations for the learnings table so the Librarian can create, read, update, and supersede learnings.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/librarian/learnings-db.ts
Implement insertLearning(), getLearning(), updateLearning(), supersedeLearning(), searchLearnings(filters)
searchLearnings supports filtering by category, project_type, and source_agent
All functions use the existing getDb() from src/db.ts
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow the same patterns as existing db.ts functions (prepare statements, typed returns).

userStories.title: Create learning extractor module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a module that uses Claude to extract structured learnings from raw agent output text.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/librarian/extractor.ts
Implement extractLearnings(rawText, sourceAgent) that spawns claude -p with an extraction prompt
The extraction prompt asks Claude to identify specific, reusable learnings and return them as a JSON array of strings
Parses Claude's JSON output into string[]
Handles parse failures gracefully (returns empty array)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Use a simple claude -p call via execFile, not the full spawnClaudeProcess. Keep the timeout short (2 min).

userStories.title: Create Librarian decision module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a module that decides whether a new learning should be filed, merged with an existing one, or skipped.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/librarian/decision.ts
Implement decideLearning(newLearning, similarLearnings) that spawns claude -p with the Librarian decision prompt
Returns a decision: SKIP | UPDATE(existingId) | REPLACE(existingId) | FILE_NEW with extracted metadata (category, tags, project_type)
Handles parse failures gracefully (defaults to FILE_NEW)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The decision prompt provides the new learning and top similar existing learnings, asks Claude to decide in JSON.

userStories.title: Create Librarian public API (research + fileLearnings)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a clean public API that orchestrates the Librarian's read and write flows.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/librarian/index.ts
Implement research({ taskDescription, projectType, limit }) that embeds the query, searches the vector store, filters by metadata, and returns Learning[]
Implement fileLearnings({ rawText, sourceAgent, projectType, repoUrl, taskId }) that extracts learnings, deduplicates via decision module, and stores them
Both functions are no-ops when LIBRARIAN_ENABLED is false
Both functions handle errors gracefully (log and continue, never crash the task)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This ties together embeddings, vectorstore, learnings-db, extractor, and decision modules.

userStories.title: Integrate Librarian into kickoff phase
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the kickoff phase to query the Librarian for relevant learnings and inject them into the prompt, and extract learnings from the output afterward.
acceptanceCriteria: Modify buildKickoffPrompt() to accept an optional learnings parameter and render them as a 'Relevant Learnings' section
In executor.ts, call research() before kickoff and pass results to buildKickoffPrompt()
In executor.ts, call fileLearnings() after kickoff with the output
Existing behavior is unchanged when LIBRARIAN_ENABLED is false
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The learnings section should go after the task details but before the instructions.

userStories.title: Integrate Librarian into Ralph loop
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Ralph loop to receive relevant learnings before starting and extract learnings from progress.txt afterward.
acceptanceCriteria: In executor.ts, before runRalphLoop(), call research() and write results to scripts/ralph/learnings.md in the target repo
Update scripts/ralph/CLAUDE.md to instruct the agent to read learnings.md for cross-task context
In executor.ts, after runRalphLoop(), read progress.txt from the repo and call fileLearnings()
Existing behavior is unchanged when LIBRARIAN_ENABLED is false
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: learnings.md is written once before the loop starts. It gets cleaned up with the rest of ralph scaffolding.

userStories.title: Integrate Librarian into review phase
userStories.description: As a developer, I want PR reviews to benefit from and contribute to the Librarian's knowledge base.
acceptanceCriteria: In review/executor.ts, call research() before running the review and inject learnings into the review prompt
After review completes, call fileLearnings() with the review output
Existing behavior is unchanged when LIBRARIAN_ENABLED is false
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Review learnings are especially valuable - they capture code quality patterns and common mistakes.

userStories.title: Add learnings admin API endpoints
userStories.description: As an admin, I want API endpoints to view and manage learnings from the admin dashboard.
acceptanceCriteria: Add GET /api/learnings endpoint that lists learnings with optional filters (category, project_type, source_agent) and pagination
Add DELETE /api/learnings/:id endpoint to remove a learning
Add GET /api/learnings/stats endpoint that returns counts by category and source_agent
Endpoints require admin auth (same as existing admin API)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow the same patterns as existing admin API in src/admin/api.ts.
